entry.title: Standard content for Asset management
entry.content:
Hi,

I am looking for some easy to configure standard content packages for Asset management. I have gone through the standard content SAP help page and I found only one which is having SAP Datasphere as a source.

We are using S/4HANA Cloud as a source

If anyone can suggest any other packages for this, it will be a great help.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Adobe Form table with Header and Item Data : Nested Tables
entry.content: This is very easy to do within the context tab to match corresponding using the "Where conditions"

entry.title: Exploring the Future: Thoughts on AI in PLM
entry.content:
Welcome to our latest video discussion where I had the chance to ask industry experts to share their thoughts on the fusion of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Product Lifecycle Management (PLM) at the Hannover Messe 2024. In this insightful conversation, they share their visions and insights into how AI is poised to transform PLM processes, driving efficiency, innovation, and productivity to new heights.

Meet the Experts: Joining us in this discussion are:

  • Dominik Mark Metzger, Head of Digital Supply Chain Solutions at SAP
  • Felix Finster, Senior Solution Manager at CIDEON Software & Services
  • Sebastian Nolting, Solution Advisor for SAP PLM at SAP
  • Andreas J Wagner, Chief Strategy & Market Development Officer at SAP
  • Marcus Kranz, Managing Director of Karon Beratungsgesellschaft mbH
  • David Kramp, Director of Sales at LeverX
  • Stefan Ziegler, Regional Business Development Manager PLM at SAP, MEE

Key Insights: Here are some highlights from our discussion:

  1. Harnessing Unstructured Data: Dominik Mark Metzger discusses the potential of AI in leveraging unstructured data within PLM, revolutionizing the application landscape and expediting the idea-to-market process.
  2. Personalization with Historical Data: Felix Finster emphasizes the importance of utilizing historical data to personalize AI, making PLM processes more efficient and tailored to individual company needs.
  3. AI in Product Innovation: Sebastian Nolting outlines various applications of AI in product innovation, from generating product ideas to combining CAD data with installed-based data seamlessly.
  4. Data as the Future of PLM: Andreas J Wagner underlines the significance of data in driving AI's impact on PLM, highlighting its role in faster product development, requirement gathering, and user interface enhancements.
  5. Automatization and Productivity: Marcus Kranz identifies AI as the next big thing in PLM, foreseeing increased productivity through automatization of tasks such as product structure management and design tagging.
  6. AI in Formulation Space: David Kramp shares insights into leveraging AI in the formulation space, discussing prototypes of natural language AI search tools for enterprise processes and PLM formulation in the cloud.
  7. Future Expectations: Stefan Ziegler expresses anticipation for advancements in AI, particularly in automating change requests and process templates, hinting at exciting developments to come.

As our experts have highlighted, the integration of AI into PLM holds immense promise for businesses worldwide. 

Thank You to Our Participants

We extend our heartfelt gratitude to our esteemed panelists for their invaluable contributions to this discussion.

Explore more:

Ready to explore more about SAP PLM software and join a vibrant community of like-minded professionals? Visit our product page for in-depth information and discover how SAP PLM solutions can elevate your business to new heights. 

https://www.sap.com/products/scm/product-lifecycle-management.html  

Curious to learn what is AI features are upcoming in SAP PLM? Check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to dive in yourself!

https://roadmaps.sap.com/business-views/E739C76B38B41EEF80F45A700F77F097

Looking for a one-stop shop to stay updated with the latest news, insights, and discussions in the world of SAP PLM? Join our community and connect with industry experts, share your experiences, and gain valuable knowledge to drive success in your organization. 

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/product-lifecycle-management  

And don't forget to subscribe to our SAP Community YouTube Channel or join our LinkedIn Expert Forum for more engaging videos, insights, and behind-the-scenes looks at the evolution of PLM software at SAP. 

https://www.youtube.com/@SAPCommunity/videos  

https://www.linkedin.com/groups/9257139/

entry.title: Re: SSAM 2405 App update causses issue
entry.content: You should be able to run the 2310 version of metadata using the 2405 client. The only step you need to do is to push an app update on the mobile service so that you can get the prompt on the device for the App Update. Once you press "Now", the app update will change the destination to 2310 and it will be business as usual.

entry.title: ME05: I need to update the "valid to" date in the source list for 400 MM numbers.
entry.content: There is currently a source list, I need to update the "valid to" date.  In the test mode the source list is updated BUT the scheduling agreement number, SA line number and the MRP "2" does not populate. What needs to be done so these field are populated? ME05 test:1st line is good, second line missing SA#, item# and MRP"2"

entry.title: Re: Make a Custom Report from Standard SAP Report
entry.content: Hello , thanks for your reply. You said that is Bad idea to copy as a standard Report, but the point is , Can i do this ?

entry.title: Re: Pick lists into one A/R Invoice
entry.content: You would need to review the split criteria for invoices.

entry.title: What replaces MB1B (ECC) multiple material to plant transfer in S/4Hana?
entry.content: We used to have MB1B where we were able to transfer multiple materials in the same screen (see below). What replaces this in S/4Hana? I've tried Post goods movement but can't find any option to copy and paste like we did in ECC....Please help...thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP Formula similar to excel CEILING function
entry.content:
I don't know how to delete the question, so I'm replying with the formula that I came up with, in case someone else will be running into the same question in the future:

 

IF(KF_A<=Attribute_B, Attribute_B",CEIL(KF_A/Attribute_B)*"Attribute_B")

entry.title: Create non-ABAP SAP systems manually in SAP Cloud ALM Landscape Management
entry.content:
Hi SAP

We currently have various non-ABAP SAP systems (LMLS, Convergent Charging, BOXI, TREX, etc.) which we would like to include as virtual systems in Landscape Management in SAP Cloud ALM.
These should only be used for business service management. Unfortunately, this is not described anywhere in the SAP Help Expert Portal.
Thanks for the support and the tip on how I can do this.

Best regards


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Create non-ABAP SAP systems manually in SAP Cloud ALM...
entry.content:
Full information is available in KBA 3479532 - How to get non-ABAP SAP systems in SAP Cloud ALM Landscape Management?

In short, if the SAP Cloud ALM list of Supported Solutions or the Setup Managed Components (Services & Systems) guide doesn't mention your specific non-ABAP SAP system, then it cannot be entered in the Landscape Management app.

To indicate you want specific non-ABAP SAP systems to be supported, please log an SAP Customer influence campaign request for the SAP Cloud ALM campaigns, found in KBA 3391676.

entry.title: HU not created in EWM inbound
entry.content:
Hi expert,

I have distributed inbound delivery to ewm and generated batch but when i try to pack automatically seems nothing happen. Supposedly system will generate HU but nothing HU created. Please advise, do we have any solution on this?

Regards
Rafał Klimek

We want to change the price after discount value but we cant able to change it since its not active in form settings and field is not editable where as same field is editable in India Localization.

Please let me know any settings is required to make the column active please refer attachment and advise us.

Thank you @roland_szajko, test from RSA3 and RODPS_REPL_TEST are working fine.

What I'm wondering now is if I have to follow this guide: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_NETWEAVER_750/6522d0462aeb4909a79c3462b090ec51/8a4121d239094ffebac228e05771cb82.html?locale=en-US

I can't really find anything that help to implement this in community or support site..

Do you have any idea?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Make a Custom Report from Standard SAP Report
entry.content:
Of course, you can copy the program, and the class behind, in a customer namespace. Then adapt the code.

  • But you shoudln't - read 11th crime of Top 10 ABAP crimes

entry.title: Re: Can we use Super BOM and super routing in AVC?
entry.content:
Dear Joao,

yes, absolutely you can use it in S/4HANA 2023. Please also refer the the Feature Scope Descriptions in this area.

Regards, Ulf.

entry.title: Re: Is it really impossible to assign the same sales grou...
entry.content:
Hi @FR_Fujita,

Indeed, this is standard behavior. I would recommend reviewing the SAP KBA 3461668 - Unable to assign Sales Group to multiple Sales Offices

Hope this helps.

Arturo Montes de Oca

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content: Getting below error.Could you please help.

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content:
You should be able to check details for such correlation-id using DBAdmin and the query like

 

~/sap/hdbclient/hdbsql -U myDBadmin -jA "SELECT AUTHENTICATION_FOR, USER_NAME, REASON FROM SYS.AUTHENTICATION_ERROR_DETA
ILS WHERE CORRELATION_ID='55896626B5A4DC47A1D09B7F9BC1D2D5'"

entry.title: Re: Questions re: "Multi-model with SAP HANA Cloud" developer challenge
entry.content: Can you please check that your SAP HANA db is in the running state?

entry.title: Re: The Small JSON Document Store Cookbook
entry.content:
Hi @mkemeter,

thanks for this article! I have played around with the JSON Document Store some time ago and it seems that the #HANA I used then had the document store already enabled. I'm now trying to enable it on a different HANA (single container on-premise) and none of the statements I find are working. Do you -- or anyone else reading here -- have a clue?

Thanks and best regards,
Robert

entry.title: Re: NF-e Nota Técnica 2023.004 v1.10: novos campos adicionados à nota fiscal
entry.content:
Olá @jgaia.

Se você utiliza o GRC NF-e (software component SLL-NFE), sim, a implementação da SAP Note 3426933 - NF-e NT2019.001 & NT2023.004 é necessária. Esta SAP Note também está mencionada no KBA 2969814 - NF-e Technical Note 2023.004 Troubleshooting Guide.

Atte.

entry.content:
Yes Hana instance is running

 

ng.

Where should I look for the split criteria? Honestly I can't remember any kind of setup or list about split criteria.

Could you please explain it in details?

Adam

entry.title: Re: Trying to understand the behaviour of intent lock in...
entry.content: Exclusive intent locks and Exclusive row locks held by a SPID DOL tables doesn't allowing to read a row entirely different than in the update. The select request is going to lock sleep till the first process commit the tran.

entry.title: Re: Robosapiens and SAPGUI Library in Robot Framework: At...
entry.content: Thanks @stefan_schnell, I use Scripting Tracker to find the Element Id but i always get ->session.findById("wnd[0]/usr/subSCREEN_1:SAPLIQS0:1060/txtVIQMEL-QMTXT").text in the recorder. I also tried to record in SAP GUI using recording and Playback, even the same element id i can see in.VBS File. As I am new to Robotframework with robosapiens/SAp GUI Library, not sure whether you are identifying the element in similar way or in different way

entry.content: those html are for local test only, you probably never use it in production.

entry.title: Re: Transport Carbon Footprint Calculation in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: New or not to the SAP Community, share your story!
entry.content: Hi all, Ivo here, my expertise is in area of Fiori fullstack development on S/4 HANA (UI5, Fiori Elements, oData, CDS, ABAP). I thoroughly enjoy the never-ending learning and lot of new stuff coming in all these areas. I always try to find "the right" way to do things. I started with SAP back in 2008.

entry.title: Re: Pick lists into one A/R Invoice
entry.content: Is your tag correct as "SAP Business One"? If so, I can't really speak to invoice splitting.

entry.title: Re: Best Practices for using current SAP APM Rules for Condition Monitoring
entry.content: @kashani in PAI and also (planned) in the 2408 release (see for the high level architecture https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blogs-by-sap/sap-asset-performance-management-embedding-cumulocity-iot-to-drive/ba-p/13679733) you can refer to aggregates in rules. In current APM you cannot. Workaround is to push in other than the raw values also the aggregates e.g. maximum calculated by data historian.

entry.title: Re: Make a Custom Report from Standard SAP Report
entry.content: Ok, I understand what you are saying. I start to make a new program from ES_SD_SALES_DOCUMENT_VIEW and it works well. Now i know 2 technical names from 2 fields , how can i add this to my Code ?

entry.title: Re: SAP Asset Performance Management Embedding Cumulocity IoT to Drive Innovations in IoT and AI
entry.content:
One question from a customer today was: "Can I ingest for the same equipment some data via MQTT (real-time) and historic data via file upload?". I assume the answer is yes but i will check and provide an update here. Certainly the file upload is "cheaper" and simpler when sending a lot of data.

UPDATE: Our lead architect (Dev) stated that the answer is "Yes, this works."

entry.title: Space tables exist in other spaces Datasphere
entry.content:
Hey Specs !

Could you please help me out the following issue in SAP datashpere?

I am trying to use replication flow but I run on error.
Error creating replication for dataset BSEG: space tables exist in other spaces: BSEG found in 0024d3e2-9959-4d02-81ad-3f02458be850

I've checked all my replication flow but none of it contains the same table from the same .
 I am using one SLT server for multiple SAP ERP. 
I do not know if it can cause this issue but I transport BSEG table from multiple ERP systems through 1 SLT.

entry.title: Dynamic parallel processing in SAP S/4HANA flexible workflow
entry.content:
In classical workflow we had option to create parallel approval workitems based on a multiline element using something like &et_item[&_WF_PARFOREACH_INDEX&]& in binding of the activity whereas the et_item is a multiline elemet in 'Miscellaneous' tab of the activity. 

But flexible workflow is based on a workflow scenario. Is any similar feature available in flexible workflow? 

Best regards,

Sumit

entry.content:
Hi,

We have a requirement to bring QTY to BPC along with 0Amount. created a MVAL in Transformation File and it is validated successfully but triggers error saying "Mapping Table can only contain one *MVAL". We already used MVAL function for another Mapping. Is this causing the issue. How to handle this MVAL. Below is the Transformation File.

SAP Business Planning and Consolidation, version for SAP NetWeaver 

Thanks

Sekhar SR

entry.title: How to Resolve the Error : HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED in RAP Application?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I created a GSTR1 RAP application in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud. when I am selecting all the coming data( it is around 3000+ entries ) and send all the line items to another portal I am getting this below error.

Runtime Error : HTTP_MAX_CONN_EXCEEDED. The O Data request processing has been abnormally terminated.

Error Cause:- "The maximum number of HTTP client connections has been exceeded". I found that there is multiple HTTP request are generated for a Instance and I am not able to handle this case.

Thanks for your Support.

entry.title: Getting and Applying a Deprecation Trial Token for the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation
entry.content:
Browsers Are Ending Third-Party Cookie Support

Browser manufacturers are ending support of third-party cookies. Google Chrome is the most prominent example, with its third-party cookie deprecation program planned for the end of 2024. To ease the transition and give application owners more time to implement a final solution, Google offers the possibility to opt-out from the third-party cookie sunset via a deprecation trial.

In our third installment, we’ll take you through the deprecation trial process.

 

What Is a Deprecation Trial?

To help you prepare for the third-party cookie deprecation and get some more time to think of a permanent solution, Google has set up a deprecation trial to temporarily opt-out of the third-party cookie deprecation.

During this period, your application is exempt of the third-party cookie deprecation. Note that the deprecation trial will end on December 27th, 2024.

There are two types of deprecation trial:

  • Third-Party Deprecation Trial: deprecation trial for embeds.
  • First-Party Deprecation Trial: deprecation trial for top-level sites.

Depending on your scenario, you can apply for one of the other.

We recommend reading our   to help you decide what is the best option according to your scenario. This guide will help you register your solution for a deprecation trial and deploy your deprecation trial token if your solution is deemed eligible by Google.

You can register your application for either for the first-party or third-party deprecation trial via Google’s dedicated website.

 

Why Is The Deprecation Trial Token Important?

The deprecation trial token helps easing the transition and gives you more time to implement a final solution.

Make sure that you deploy your deprecation trial token by June 30th. Starting from July 1st 2024, origins registered for the deprecation trial will no longer have access to third-party cookies in Chrome if they have not deployed their tokens.

Check out our full guide covering the deprecation trial token deployment.

If you are still unsure whether your solution is affected, check out our first blog. If you are affected and want to start testing, check out our second blog.

This blog is the third part of our series covering the third-party cookie deprecation:

  • Are You Affected by the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation? 
  • How to test your solution for breakage? Full testing guide is now available: Preparing and Testing Your Solution for Third-Party Cookie Deprecation .
  • How to handle deprecation trial tokens? Full deployment guide is now available: Getting and Applying a Deprecation Trial Token for the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation
  • How to prepare your application for the blockage of third-party cookies. (Coming Soon)

Keep watching this space for more information regarding Google’s third-party cookie deprecation.

entry.title: Dynamic Textfield with hight greater 1 in Dynpro.
entry.content:
Hey,
I'm trying to create a screen with dynamic text filling. The screen will later run on a service on an MDT, which is why I have a fixed width of 20 characters.
But my dynamic texts will definitely have more than 20 characters.
So I would like a text field that has a height greater than 1.
But I can't set the height in the Screen Painter.
Is there a way to do this without using a text control? This is not compatible with access via service.

Thank you very much for your answers!

 

Best wishes,
David

entry.title: Re: Pick lists into one A/R Invoice
entry.content:
Yes, SAP Business One related.

Maybe I picked up a wrong group when I created my question, but this site is like trash, you just can't choose what you really need when you post a question...

Adam

entry.title: SAP Sybase: select for update + readpast
entry.content:
Following queries fetching same set of records despite using select for update and readpast clauses. Same behavior when query is executed by 2 different threads.

BEGIN TRANSACTION;
-- First query to lock rows
SELECT TOP 50 emp_id, emp_name, emp_flag 
FROM poll_qa_tbl READPAST
WHERE ((emp_flag <> 'POLL') OR (emp_flag IS NULL)) FOR UPDATE of emp_flag;
-- Second query to fetch rows not yet locked
SELECT TOP 50 emp_id, emp_name, emp_flag 
FROM poll_qa_tbl READPAST
WHERE ((emp_flag <> 'POLL') OR (emp_flag IS NULL)) FOR UPDATE of emp_flag;
COMMIT TRANSACTION;

Thanks,
Ankit

entry.title: Re: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp...

entry.title: Unable to display SQL trace in ABAP Profiler
entry.content:
Hi community,

I've traced a background process with ABAP profiler, with the "Enable SQL Trace" option active in preferences tab.

But when I get into the trace, in the overview tab I can't see the SQL trace from Analysis Tools

 



Do you know if there's any configuration missing? Thanks

entry.title: CDS View for the WorkPackages Tables in the Plan Customer Projects App
entry.content:
Hello everyone. 

I' am searching a View that shows me the same information that are visible in the table from the Work Packages in the App "Plan Customer Projects".

I have searched for many views but I haven't found nothing that shows me the Planned Revenue on the Basis of the Work Packages in an Customer Project. 

Does anyone know what is the name of the CDS-View that has this information or does anyone know how i can get this informations?.

Thank you very much for your help.

Best regards,
Simone

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP - Cloud vs ON premise
entry.content: Via BTP does UX innovation, machine learning, artificial intelligence or chatbots are only available through cloud? or are they also available for the on premise solution?

entry.title: Re: DataSphere "Hierarchy with Directory" configuration p...
entry.content:
Hello hleboeuf,

Whatever table or view you use as an association in your model needs to be of "Semantic Usage" = Dimension.  By default it is "Relational Dataset".

Scott

entry.title: PBO FLOW LOGIC FOR TABLECONTROL - change some fields in table editable
entry.content:
I'm looking help for table on subscreen. Some fields (not colums) in table I need to be editable. 

In case in value field I have number > 3 this field should be editable, SCRREN-INPUT = 1. But it doesn't work.

 

LOOP AT gt_on_screen INTO gs_on_screen 
    WITH CONTROL on_screen 
    CURSOR on_screen-current_line 
 MODULE on_screen_get_lines.
 FIELD gs_on_screen-field1 MODULE input_off.
ENDLOOP.

MODULE input_off.
  LOOP AT SCREEN.
    IF screen-name = 'GS_ON_SCREEN-NO' AND GS_ON_SCREEN-NO > 3.
      screen-input = 1.
      MODIFY SCREEN.
    ENDIF.
  ENDLOOP.
ENDMODULE.

entry.title: AI Adoption und AI Transformation – von der Technik zur erfolgreichen Nutzung, Anpassung Veränderung
entry.content:
Wir erleben aktuell besondere Zeiten der mehrfachen Disruption. So wird künstliche Intelligenz fundamental nicht nur Technologie, sondern auch die Arbeit wandeln. Wie gut uns dabei die Transformation gelingt, hängt auch von unseren Vorgehensweisen bei der Adoption, Adaption und Transformation ab. Worüber sprechen wir da aber eigentlich?

„In computing, adoption means the transfer (conversion) between an old system and a target system in an organization (or more broadly, by anyone). If a company works with an old software system, it may want to use a new system which is more efficient, has more work capacity, etc. So then a new system needs to be adopted, after which it can be used by users.“

Sieht man es ganzheitlicher, geht es über die eher „technische“  Einführung von Software hinaus, um eine möglichst umfassende Akzeptanz und Produktivität von Nutzern und Nutzerinnen hinsichtlich digitalen Werkzeuge zu erzielen. Wie immer kommt es dabei eigentlich auf die diversen Perspektiven der Stakeholder an:

  • das Management will ROI, Kostensparung, Wachstum, Produktivität,
  • die IT möchte niedrige Integrationskosten bei hoher Sicherheit,
  • Fachbereiche wünschen sich fachliche Ziele und
  • die eigentlichen NutzerInnen wünschen sich wenig Frustration und dass ihre Arbeit einfacher, schneller oder besser wird.

Das sind alles Wünsche oder Projektionen, die es jenseits der reinen technologischen Perspektive zu berücksichtigen gilt, besonders auch für Anwendungen generativer künstlichen Intelligenz. Gerade hier ist jedoch die Akzeptanz sowie produktive Nutzung zentral. Denn bei der Einführung und Nutzung von KI schwingen viele Ängste mit – sie erstrecken sich von Dystopien wie der Auslöschung der Welt über Unsicherheit bis hin zur Angst vor einem individuellen Job- und Relevanzverlust. Daher soll hier Adoption auch erweitert als Reifungsprozess über die technische Nutzung oder Prozessoptimierung hinaus verstanden und auch aus „menschlicher Perspektive“ analysiert werden.

Die Digitale Adoption als Entwicklungsprozess

Ich habe dafür im Rahmen der Digital Adoption Blogging Challenge in Anlehnung an Maslow als schon unterschieden zwischen klassischen Adoption als Nutzung bis hin zur begeisterten Nutzung. 

In dem genannten Beitrag wurde beschrieben, dass wir vor allem mit den obersten Stufen das engere Verständnis von Adoption nicht nur „affektiv“ erweitern, sondern vor allem auch beim Grad der Reifung in Richtung Adaption und Transformation, denn am Ende wollen wir jetzt auch bei der Künstlichen Intelligenz mehr erreichen als nur eine leidenschaftslose Nutzung von Tools. Im Idealfall wird eine technologische Chance umfassend in die eigene Wertschöpfung integriert bis hin zur begeisterten Transformation von Wertschöpfung – d.h. Prozesse, Geschäftsmodelle, Rollen bis Aufgaben neu denken.

Die KI-Transformation braucht die erweiterte digital Adoption

Auf Basis dieser generellen Reflektionen zur Adoption, Adaption und Transformation wollen wir nun die KI-Transformation und KI Adoption beleuchten.  Wesentlichen Erfolgsbausteine sind jenseits der rein technologischen Fragen wie üblich Kommunikation, Einbindung, klare Ziele, Leitplanken, Lernangebote, Verstehen der Widerstände, Motivationen, Interessen und andere Ansätze – also Bausteine die man generell aus dem Change Management kennt. Sie sind essenziell, um Ängsten oder Widerständen, wie sie aktuell im Kontext KI entstehen, zu begegnen.

Welche Vorgehensweisen und Modelle können helfen?

Im Folgenden versuche ich etablierte Modelle neu zu kontextualisieren für die jetzt anstehende KI-Transformation. Natürlich sind auch andere Klassiker auch im neuen Kontext hilfreich. Hier seien beispielhaft einige vorgestellt:

Ein vereinfachtes Modell ist das des Sollen, Können, Dürfen und Wollen:

  • Sollen”: Gemeint ist eine klare KI Strategie und ein Ziel, wie man einen Erfolg genau definieren würde. Bei KI ist wichtig, unter den vielen Use Cases diejenigen zu identifizieren, die derzeit technisch umsetzbar sind, akzeptiert sind und den höchsten Mehrwert bringen.
  • „Können” bedeutet: Die jeweiligen KI Kompetenzen haben alle – oder können sie je nach Zielgruppe entwickeln.
  • „Dürfen“ heißt, dass die entsprechenden Befugnisse und Freiräume vorhanden sind um z.B. KI Tools wie Co-Piloten, Agenten oder Chatbots zu nutzen.
  • „Wollen“ bedeutet, dass Überzeugung und Akzeptanz zur Nutzung von KI vorherrschen.

Das „Diffusionsmodell“ von Everett Rogers beschreibt, wie Innovationen in einer Organisation aufgenommen und verbreitet werden. Es identifiziert fünf Typen von Menschen (Innovatoren, frühe Adopter, frühe Mehrheit, späte Mehrheit, Nachzügler) und deren unterschiedliche Bereitschaft zur Technologieannahme. Für die Nutzung und Akzeptanz von generativer Künstlicher Intelligenz im Unternehmen weist es auf die Notwendigkeit hin, die Mitarbeiter über die Technologie zu informieren, zu schulen und fortlaufende Unterstützung anzubieten. Dazu passt das nächste Modell.

"Crossing the Chasm" von Geoffrey Moore befasst sich mit der Herausforderung, eine innovative Technologie von einer kleinen Gruppe früher Adopter zu einer breiten Mehrheit zu vermitteln. Es empfiehlt, sich auf eine spezifische Zielgruppe zu konzentrieren und sicherzustellen, dass die Technologie an sich ändernde Kundenerwartungen angepasst werden kann. Für generative KI bedeutet dies, dass verschiedene Zielgruppen unterschiedlich behandelt werden sollten. So kann man die Early Adopter und Innovatoren als Pilot-Teilnehmer oder als Promotoren nutzen, z.B. für Change Agent Netzwerke oder Coaches.

Die disruptive Innovation von Clayton M. Christensen zeigt, wie neue Technologien alte Prozesse ersetzen, indem sie einfacher und kostengünstiger sind. Die Aktzeptanz von KI im Unternehmen kann dadurch erhöht werden, indem man eine Kultur des Experimentierens und Lernens fördert und KI  aktiv in jedem Team einführt um bestehende Aufgaben und Prozesse zu optimieren oder sogar zu ersetzen.

Das Technologieakzeptanz-modell (TAM) erklärt, wie Benutzer eine neue Technologie annehmen und verwenden, basierend auf ihrer wahrgenommenen Nützlichkeit und Benutzerfreundlichkeit. Es unterstreicht die Notwendigkeit, bei der Einführung von generativer KI im Unternehmen deutlich zu machen, wie sie die Arbeit effizienter macht und wie einfach sie zu bedienen ist. Das TAM ist das wohl am besten empirisch untersuchte und erweiterte Modell - so wurden auch weitere beeinflussende Variablen ausdifferenziert wie sozialer Einfluss (Subjektive Norm, Image und Freiwilligkeit ) und kognitive Prozesse (Sozialer Einfluss, kognitiv-instrumentelle Prozessvariablen). Neuer Untersuchungen stellen zunehmend das Vertrauen der Nutzer in die Technologie als akzeptanzfördernd hervor – gerade bei KI ein zentraler Punkt.

Das ADKAR-Modell ist ein weiteres bekanntes Framework für Change-Management. Es zeigt, wie Individuen erfolgreich Veränderungen durchlaufen können, und kann zur Förderung der Akzeptanz von KI im Unternehmen angewendet werden. Dabei geht es um Awareness (Bewusstsein schaffen), Desire (den Wunsch der Veränderung schaffen), Knowledge (Wissen vermitteln), Ability (Fähigkeiten ausbauen) und Reinforcement (Verstärkung). Es gilt dabei, keines der Elemente zu vergessen bei der Einführung von KI und zeigt, daß Awareness nicht gleich Knowledge oder Ability ist und die Verstetigung durch Verstärkung und kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung wichtig ist.

Kotters Modell für "Leading Change" besteht aus acht Schritten zur Schaffung erfolgreicher Veränderungen. Es kann die Akzeptanz von generativer KI fördern, indem es eine strategische Vision und ein Narrativ für die Nutzung der KI und ihrer Vorteile erstellt und kommuniziert, Koalitionen schmiedet, Hindernisse beseitigt und Quick-Wins erreicht.

Die Veränderungskurve nach Kübler-Ross beschreibt die emotionalen Phasen, die Menschen bei Veränderungen erleben. Sie kann bei der Implementierung von generativer KI helfen, indem sie die unterschiedlichen emotionalen Reaktionen der Mitarbeiter berücksichtigt um danach Kommunikation und Unterstützung anzupassen. Die Phasen variieren und sind u.a. Vorahnung, Schock, Ablehnung, Verzweiflung im Tal der Tränen, Lernen und Anpassung, Akzeptanz und Integration. Betroffene durchlaufen diese Phasen in ganz unterschiedlicher Geschwindigkeit oder je nach Kontext ganz anders. Es zeigt, dass Emotionen wie Angst, Furcht, Begeisterung, Neid immer bei Veränderungen mitspielen und je nach Stakeholder zu prüfen und zu behandeln sind.  

SAP Activate ist eine Methodik zur Implementierung von SAP-Lösungen, in die ja KI zunehmend eingebettet ist. Es bietet viele Templates und Tools, um SAP Projekte zu erleichtern. Dabei gibt es u.a. den Workstream "Solution Adoption", der auf die Bedürfnisse von Change Management und Weiterbildung eingeht.

Bei allen Frameworks und Modellen ist es natürlich wichtig empathisch und intuitiv auch auf die jeweiligen Interessen, Emotionen und Kultur zu reagieren - je danach was eben im Kontext Sinn macht. Die Frameworks sind wie Werkzeugkisten – gut ist wenn man sie kennt und versteht. Abe im Zweifel eben nicht oder anders benutzt.

Im Folgenden der Versuch einer Zusammenfassung wichtiger Bausteine, speziell für den Bereich der AI Adoption.

Bausteine einer erfolgreichen humanzentrierten AI Adoption

In der nachfolgenden Abbildung findet man angedeutet vier wesentliche Bausteine einer humanzentrierten KI/AI Adoption jenseits der rein technischen Betrachtung mit einigen wichtigen Stichpunkten dazu incl. Links für den SAP Bereich. Auch wenn hier eine gewisse Chronologie wahrscheinlich ist, stehen diese 4 Bausteine hier doch gleichberechtigt „nebeneinander“. Diese wurden auch in der Blogging Challenge zu Digital Adoption im SAP Kontext bestätigt.

 

Human Centered Design Mindset & Praxis

Hier sind etablierte Ansätze des agilen humanzentrierten Designs von neuen Lösungen eine wesentliche Basis für den gewünschten Adoption / Adaptions / Transformations-Erfolg. Für die Auswahl der geeigneten KI Szenarien hilft dabei zum Beispiel das SAP Apphaus Toolkit für generative AI – der generative AI Exploration Workshops. Ob Design Thinking, Co-Creation, Experimentieren, agiles Coaching oder andere Ansätze – wichtig ist immer den Nutzer in den Mittelpunkt zu stellen und von ihm und ihr aus zu denken. 

Organizational Change Management

Jenseits der individuellen Perspektive bedeutet eine AI Adoption in den höheren Stufen immer auch Change Management. Dabei ist u.a. folgendes wichtig:

Klarheit und Nutzen: Vorteile der neuen Technologie müssen klar kommuniziert werden je nach Rolle oder Persona. Mitarbeitende sollten verstehen, wie die Technologie ihre Arbeit effizienter/besser macht.

Vertrauen und einfache Bedienbarkeit hilft zur Akzeptanz: Das humanzentrierte Design vom Nutzer aus, ein einfacher Zugang und die Benutzerfreundlichkeit der Technologie sind von entscheidender Bedeutung. Das Vertrauen in die Tools wird dabei zunehmend wichtig. Besonders bei KI sind vertrauensfördernde Maßnahmen wie Transparenz der Modelle, oder Prüfung von möglichem Bias wichtig.

SAP bietet hier kostenfreie Tools, Communities sowie Organizational Change Management Services an wie den datengestütze Deep Transformation Insights Service.  

Learning and Enablement – auch KI Upskilling genannt

Die wichtigsten KI-Investitionen sind derzeit neben Automatisierung zur Erzielung von höherer Produktivität und Kosten-Effizienz die Umschulung oder das Upskilling.

Neben Grundlagen  geht es dabei immer um den Einsatz von generativer KI im jeweiligen Arbeitsalltag und Job. Für die Grundlagen bieten sich e-learning Angebote an. Einige Inhalte sind auch kostenfrei, zum Beispiel über das HPI oder den KI-Campus. Für wichtige Zielgruppen wie Software-Entwicklung, F&E oder IT werden oft auch spezielle firmeninterne Akademien aufgebaut.

Da das Ziel des Upskilling immer Handlungskompetenz und die konkrete Umsetzung ist, sollte KI Training über Grundlagen hinaus gehen, Üben, Ausprobieren, Reflektieren beinhalten und im Austausch mit anderen stattfinden. Mögliche Methoden sind dabei Workshops oder Hackathons, in denen an relevanten Herausforderungen gearbeitet wird. Im LernOS Framework gibt es zudem einen KI-Leitfaden. Dieser Leitfaden strukturiert eine selbstgesteuerte Lernreise von Kleingruppen mit Input und Übungen rund um KI. Damit kann das Lernen am Arbeitsplatz und der Austausch einfach und relativ kostenneutral realisiert werden.

Mitarbeitende können zum kontinuierlichen Lernen auch ChatGPT & Co. als persönliche Lerntutoren nutzen.  
SAP KI Produkte kann man über dem SAP Learning Hub oder über SAP Schulungen lernen. Für das Befähigen von Endanwendern bietet sich SAP Enable Now an, das auch zunehmend KI Features hat.

Technical Support & Consulting

Neben dem technischen „low“ und „high touch“ Support bei der Einführung kommt der laufenden Beratung eine Schlüsselrolle zu auch über den klassischen Projekt-Lebenszyklus hinaus. Da KI bei SAP Produkten eingebettet ist von HCM bis SCM oder BTP bietet sich Unterstützung in der Cloud als Support Abo mit SAP Preferred Success an oder in der private Cloud mit SAP Cloud Application Services.

Um Vertrauen zu fördern und Missbrauch zu vermeiden sollte die Nutzung von KI im Unternehmen immer entlang ethischer Grundsätze und Leitlinien laufen – die konkret operationalisiert werden müssen. Dabei sollte man dann beispielsweise bestimmen,

  • welche Use-Cases genauer geprüft werden müssen, z. B. bei der Verwendung von persönlichen Daten oder im Hinblick auf mögliche Bias,
  • welche Use-Cases ein No-Go sind, z. B. Überwachung oder Beeinflussung von Verhalten. 

Zum technischen Support gehören selbstverständlich auch Werkzeuge wie Digitale Adoption Plattformen (wie SAP Enable Now oder Walkme), Plattformen zur Zusammenarbeit & Kommunikation (Wikis, Foren etc.), Tools zur Befragung und zum entsprechenden Reporting.

Resüme & Aufruf

Dieser Beitrag kann das Thema der Adoption von KI / AI nur anreißen. Es wird hoffentlich die Diskussion motivieren, warum und wie wir über die rein technische Einführung hinaus den Erfolg dieser wichtigen Veränderung in unserer Zeit sicherstellen können. Vor allem ist interessant, was KI spezifisch relevant ist.

Früher oder später werden die meisten Wissensarbeiter KI Tools für mehr Produktivität oder Kreativität nutzen. Ob spezielle Tools oder die sogenannten Co-Piloten, die in bestehenden Software wie Microsoft Office eingebunden sind. Daher wird Adoption, Akzeptanz, Upskilling und mehr wie beschrieben für Wissensarbeiter über 2024 hinaus relevant sein, da das Feld der KI sich stets weiter entwickelt.

Dabei war leider kein Raum für regulative, technische und ethische Fragen, die das Thema Adoption auf eine gesellschaftliche Ebene heben. Es zeigt zudem, dass wir in einem Relaunch unserer früheren Blogparade noch einmal einige Beiträge speziell dem Thema KI Adoption widmen sollten. Wer Interesse hat, meldet sich gerne!

Denn eins ist klar: ohne Wissen oder Fähigkeiten oder mit einem nicht humanzentrierten Mindset werden KI Einführungen nicht nur weniger erfolgreich – sie können sogar schädlich sein. Lasst uns das verhindern und an einer humanzentrierten Nutzung von KI gemeinsam arbeiten.

 

Literatur

Podcasts zum Thema KI in der Weiterbildung https://podcast.opensap.info/educationnewscast/

Newsletter rund um KI in der Bildung von MIT Professor E. Mollick https://www.oneusefulthing.org/

Abgerufen am 22.01.2024 unter  https://www.multivu.com/players/English/9240059-ibm-2023-global-ai-adoption-index-report/

Jenewein, T. (2024): Digital Adoption und Adaption: Unternehmen erfolgreich digitalisieren – SAPNewsCenter. Abgerufen am 24.01.2024 unter   https://news.sap.com/germany/2024/01/digital-adoption-adaption/

Blog: Warum Digital Adoption für den Erfolg von SAP-Projekten so wichtig ist (und wie sie gelingt) https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-training-and-change-management/warum-digital-adoption-f%C3%BCr-den-erfolg-von-sap-projekten-so-wichtig-ist-und/ba-p/292402?url_id=text-de-newscenter:CRM-DX23-AAA-1904104_08

AI Ethik bei SAP: https://open.sap.com/courses/aie1-1

LernOS Leitfaden für KI https://cogneon.github.io/lernos-ai/de/ 

Harvard Business Review (2023). Reskilling in the Age of AI. Abgerufen am 25.01.2024 unter  https://hbr.org/2023/09/reskilling-in-the-age-of-ai

entry.title: Re: Creating a Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Bradley,

Its a nice blog. I created a similar hierarchy in datasphere and used it in one of the fact views. It has multiple hierarchies. It works perfectly fine

However, I keep only 1 hierarchy in Hier with Dir and create a variable on top of the object from the fact view which has an association to the Dimension View which in turn has an Hier with Dir Association to the Hier With Dir View. Now the variable does not show the Hierarchy but just the Flat presentation of the values.

Do you know if it is possible to show the hierarchy even in the variable using this method of Hierarchy creation? If not, which method of hierarchy creation is most suitable if we want the hierarchy to be visible even in variables.

Your inputs are much appreciated.

Thx,

Rohan

entry.title: UX Innovation on SAP BTP - Cloud vs On Premise Solution
entry.content:
In BTP does UX innovations, Application Development (Low-code/no-code tool), Automation, machine learning, artificial intelligence, chatbots, Data analytics  are only available through cloud? or are they also be integrated for the on premise solution?

What would be the difference between Cloud and on premise solution?

Is there any of the of the above that are only limited to cloud solution and not available on premise?

entry.title: Maximum number of concurrent unattended execution reached : Error in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Hi ,

I have SAP Build Process automation with Free Tier. I have created a basic job for extracting details from PDF and returning the raw information. When I run/ test the automation separately I can see the results. But when I deploy it and trigger the Process I am getting "Maximum number of concurrent unattended execution reached" error in the Monitoring tab.

I have attached images for reference

Tagging experts

@Archana 
@Chaitanya_Priya

entry.title: Re: Why the diff between index src in sap capm sapui5 app...

entry.title: Error: Failed to execute module factory for ''sap/gantt/library.js'
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have followed the below tutorial to create a Fiori Elements application.

Create an Incident Management App with SAP Fiori Elements for OData V4

I can execute the project locally, but after deploying it to BTP Cloud Foundry, I am getting the below error. This comes when I try to open the object page. And the object page does not load.

 

Any idea here how to resolve? Below is the code repository:

CSPanda21/CAP_FioriElement: CAP project with Fiori elements (github.com)

  SAP Fiori Elements   SAP Fiori Launchpad    SAP Fiori Cloud  

ERROR:

Failed to resolve dependencies of 'sap/gantt/simple/GanttChartContainer.js'
-> 'sap/gantt/library.js'
-> 'sap/gantt/config/Mode.js': failed to execute module factory for ''sap/gantt/library.js'
-> 'sap/gantt/config/Mode.js'': Failed to execute 'sap/gantt/config/Mode.js': Refused to evaluate a string as JavaScript because 'unsafe-eval' is not an allowed source of script in the following Content Security Policy directive: "script-src * data: blob:".

entry.title: Re: PBO FLOW LOGIC FOR TABLECONTROL - change some fields in table editable
entry.content: Try to remove the FIELD statement or use the CONTROLS and not a LOOP AT SCREEN

entry.title: I can't call any new functions (RFC) -- but old ones work fine. What might be happening?
entry.content:
I'm working in a new environment (S4) and we have several objects imported from the old environment. Among these objects are several RFCs.

From the CPI (Neo) I can call any of these RFCs without any problems, but I cannot call any new RFC created directly in the environment. I've already tried the following:

  • Create a new function in an old function's group.
  • Copy an existing function -- either to the same function's group or in any other new FG.

In both cases the new functions are never called via CPI. The same error occurs in all of them: "Error getting function". Of course all of them are active and also working fine if I test them locally.

This is not a problem with the Cloud Connector because there is communication with the environment and because I am using the Z prefix to be able to call any function. As I said, any and all Z functions, even if not explicitly entered into the CC, are called successfully.
I've never seen anything similar. Any idea what might be happening?

How did you resolve the error .

Here I want to refresh my reuse component to get the new values from list report. Also you mentioned to use createcontent. I want to create dynamic table based on oData. Could you please guide here? It will really helpful. Thanks in advance. Yogesh

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation - Introducing Environments
entry.content: It seems like there are some random bugs when trying to add User Groups to Shared Environments.... Sometimes changes are not saved. There are no problems with individual users.

entry.title: Hierarchy with Directory Hierarchy in Datasphere to be used in variables
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have created a hierarchy with the Hierarchy with Directory method in Datasphere. The hierarchy works in SAC as expected.

However, we want to display the hierarchy in variables created in Analytic Model on top of the Dimension which has the Hierarchy with Directory association. When we try it we don't see the hierarchy in variables. Its just a flat presentation. The hierarchy created has only 1 hierarchy.

Is it possible to display the hierarchical structure in variables created in Analytic model if the hierarchy is created using Hierarchy with Directory method? Which is the best suitable way to create a hierarchy so we can show hierarchy structure in variables created in Analytic model.

Any inputs are much appreciated.

Thank You,

Rohan

entry.title: Re: I want to send only today's files to sftp using sftp sender adapter which polls every 10mins
entry.content: You can't use a camel expression that I'm aware of inside the sender directly. Your only option would be to try and use a Poll Enrich step with reference to header "CamelFileNameOnly" as I had previously mentioned.  You would need a start timer and a content modifier to populate the "CamelFileNameOnly" header dynamically followed by a Poll Enrich step with an SFTP sender.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration - How to use PGP Keys Monitor
entry.content:
Hi @MarcoFreischlag,

We uploaded our PGP private key to PGP Keys section of CPI. Key shows Validity Until as Never Expires, however Validity State show Expired. Do you have any idea why is it showing such conflicting information?

Regards,

Netrey

entry.title: Re: Stock movement error resolution error
entry.content:
Hi There,

I don't know what movement type you are executing, but perhaps this could be caused by a mismatch between serial number stock data and stock data in material document posting.  Make sure you are moving it correctly.  

Hope this helps,

Marco

entry.title: Re: First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher)
entry.content:
Hello Goran,
According to the screenshot Actions per Role in Features from section 6. SAP Cloud ALM roles, someone with only role Project Member, under User Management tile, Roles-> Implementation -> Project Management, can do everything except create transports and deploy transports.

For the moment under User Management tile, Roles-> Implementation -> Change & Deployment Management we can select roles:
- Change manager: which allows a user to select action Approve for Production
- Deployment manager: which allows a user to deploy transports into both test and production systems or into only test systems or only into production system

The change manager and deployment manager role needs to be combined with either the project member or the project lead role.

"2nd person can approve it" -> the Project Lead and Project Admin are the only roles that allows to run actions Start Implementation (status In Implemetnation) and Handover to Test (status In Testing)

In Q22024 is expected to get a "New feature status to confirm successful test" according to the roadmap https://roadmaps.sap.com/board?PRODUCT=73554900100800002513&range=CURRENT-LAST#Q2%202024

Please check the last webinar for SAP Cloud ALM from June 5, Moritz was speaking about this new role in Deployment, the recording will be available here soon https://events.sap.com/sap-alm-community-call/en/replays

But this is the authorizations granularity delivered from the moment.

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: How to capture the event of a user pressing Ctrl+A to clear a SAP B1 form's fields
entry.content:
I am trying to capture the event of a user pressing Ctrl+A to clear a SAP Business One form's fields when that form is in focus/active.

I have tried the below but it is not catching the event.

if (pVal.FormTypeEx == "CDD" && pVal.EventType == SAPbouiCOM.BoEventTypes.et_KEY_DOWN && pVal.Modifiers == SAPbouiCOM.BoModifiersEnum.mt_CTRL && pVal.CharPressed == 065)
{
    //code
}

How do I accomplish this?

Regards,

entry.title: Re: 42 Berlin Tech Education Summit - Summary
entry.content:
Hi @andre_biener ,

Thanks for sharing! Amongst many newcomers to the SAP ecosystem, there is this perception of potential employers never quite being open to offering "entry level" positions - instead it is a position asking for 4-6 years of experience at entry-level pay. I've seen anecdotes of this, but cannot really tell if it is a justified perception or not.

Was this discussed at all during the session? I'd be curious to get the industry's perspective on this.

entry.title: How to prevent a sales order in C4C to retransfer to S4 when "Send E-mail" is used?
entry.content:
Hello,

Let us say, we have a sales order in C4C that has been transferred to S4 and got confirmed with an S4 ID.

If we use the function "Send E-Mail", the order is set to "In Process" and transferred to S4 again. 

is there a way to prevent this?

thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP WEB IDE Orion Crashing
entry.content:
Hi,

 Can you please extract the webide zip in the following path with  an extractor like 7zip:

C:\SAPWebIDE

entry.title: Re: Virtual element not refreshed on Save RAP SAP
entry.content:
Hi Wolfgang,

Thanks for your reply.

I just need to refresh the page. Do you have any blog or steps how to reload/refresh the page on save for Non-Draft.

Regards,

Santosh F.

entry.title: Notification of leaver action, before workflow approval takes place
entry.content:
Hello,

I am trying to find a solution to notify our IT team when a leaver action has been triggered for any employee in employee central; we do already have a 2 step workflow approval set up (which IT are not a part of and don’t need to be) - but we want to notify them BEFORE the workflow is approved as it can sometime be delayed causing potential security risks, and we may need to remove IT access from the leaver before their leaving date. I also want to be able to use a custom notification with specific information on it to send to IT (i.e. not use the standard notification template).

  • I don’t want to add them as an additional approver as this could slow the process even further and there is no approval required by them - is just for visibility that a leaver action has been triggered.
  • I have tried adding them as a contributor to the workflow, but that will only allow the standard notification template to be used.
  • I have tried adding multiple workflows to the leaver action - but that is not possible (as per SAP notes https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3286215#:~:text=Resolution,one%20workflow%20triggering%20per%20transaction)
  • Reporting is too late - we need to know as the action is being triggered not after the fact.

Does anyone else have a similar requirement or know of anything else I can try?

Many thanks in advance!

Liz

entry.title: Re: How to display additional columns in the report list of a Sales Fiori app?
entry.content: How to reset to default shows additonal fields?

entry.title: Re: SAP FIORI LAUNCH PAD URL
entry.content: Please run the t-code: "/n/ui2/flp" and it will open the launchpad in your default browser.

entry.title: Re: Modeling an advanced Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Stefan,

Great Blog!!

I created a similar hierarchy in datasphere and used it in one of the fact views. It has multiple hierarchies. It works perfectly fine

However, I keep only 1 hierarchy in Hier with Dir and create a variable on top of the object from the fact view which has an association to the Dimension View which in turn has an Hier with Dir Association to the Hier With Dir View. Now the variable does not show the Hierarchy but just the Flat presentation of the values.

Do you know if it is possible to show the hierarchy even in the variable using this method of Hierarchy creation? If not, which method of hierarchy creation is most suitable if we want the hierarchy to be visible even in variables.

Your inputs are much appreciated.

Thank You.

entry.title: Eclipse: How to see / change function module documentation?
entry.content: In SAPGUI there exists "Function Module Documentation". Is there a way in Eclipse to accessed and maintain it?

entry.title: SAP Developer News, June 6th, 2024
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

Code Connect 2024

  • https://code-connect.dev/

DYK #23  Bookmark for BAS dev spaces

ABAP Tools - Configure Tool Bridge

  • https://software-heroes.com/en/blog/abap-tools-work-with-ecplise-configure-tool-bridge
  • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-fiori-tools-may-2024-release-adds-closer-integration-with-abap/ba-p/13720761

Discovering Event-Driven Integration with SAP Integration Suite, AEM

  • Learning journey: https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-event-driven-integration-with-sap-integration-suite-advanced-event-mesh
  • Event-driven integrations CodeJam: https://github.com/SAP-samples/event-driven-integrations-codejam/
  • Request a CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415

ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) Cheat Sheet

  • https://d.dam.sap.com/a/UgpGkLH/Cheat_Sheet.pdf?rc=10&inline=true&doi=SAP1083288
  • https://www.brandeis.de/en/blog/cheat-sheet-cds-abap

June Developer Challenge: Multi-model in SAP HANA Cloud, incl. the Vector Engine

  • https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-blog-posts/sap-developer-challenge-sap-hana-multi-model-using-python-in-sap-business/ba-p/13722560

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:07 Code Connect 2024

0:46 DYK #23  Bookmark for BAS dev spaces

1:56 ABAP Tools - Configure Tool Bridge + SAP Fiori tools May 2024 release

3:29 Discovering Event-Driven Integration with SAP Integration Suite, AEM

4:37 ABAP Core Data Services (CDS) Cheat Sheet

5:35 June Developer Challenge: Multi-model in SAP HANA Cloud, incl. the Vector Engine

TRANSCRIPT

[DJ and Nico] Hi everyone and welcome from Code Connect 2024. It's been a fantastic week so far. We had a Cap Code Jam on Monday, we had ReCap on Tuesday, and today, what is today? It's UI5Con. It's UI5Con today. Two awesome keynote sessions already, one from Daniel yesterday and one from Peter and the gang this morning, right? And what else have we got? And we're looking forward to a lot more sessions, a lot We're networking and talking to friends and colleagues, and we hope to see you soon, next year, hopefully at Code Connect 25. Yeah, Code Connect 25, be there.

[Ajay] Did you know SAP Business Application Studio allows users to create development space tailored for specific development scenarios? When you create a dev space, it is associated with your user account, and you can access it through the BAS dashboard. If you have a bookmark to your specific DevSpace, you can indeed access it more quickly bypassing the need to navigate to the initial BAS dashboard. If your DevSpace is not running, clicking the bookmark will take you to a page where you can start the DevSpace. Starting a DevSpace can take a moment because the underlying resources need to be provisioned and initialized. If your dev space is already run in, clicking the bookmark will take you directly to the dev space environment, where you can continue with your work without any delay. Bookmarking your dev space is a convenient way to save time and reduce the number of clicks needed to access your development environment.

[Mamikee] Hello, everyone. I'm coming to you live from ABAP Conference in Germany on this beautiful Thursday, June 6th. We have a new blog post to check out. In the latest blog post, our community member speaks about the powerful tool bridge, the future in the ABAP development tools version 3.42. Learn how this integration with Business Application Studio or VS Code can revolutionize your ABAP development workflow. This blog post highlights what the tool bridge is and the significance in ABAP development, step-by-step instructions to set up the tool bridge with BAS, tips on using the tool bridge to enhance your development process, and many more. Don't miss out on this opportunity to improve your ABAP development process. Check out the link in the description below. Also, don't forget to check out this blog post by Ian Wigley where he discovers the latest enhancement in the SAP PURE tools May 2024 release, which brings closer integration with ABAP development tools with the 24.05 client release ADT and the latest SAP PURE tools. You can now enjoy a more seamless experience between backend and frontend development. In this blog post, Ian Quigley explains how the integration simplifies the development process. Read Ian Quigley's full blog post in the link below.

[Antonio] Hola, SAP developers. Are you interested in learning about Event Driven Architectures and SAP Integration Suite Advanced Vendors? Well, you're in luck, because this week we recently released a new learning journey focusing on that. learning journey is called Discovering Event-Driven Integration with SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Management. You will go through the basics on what Event-Driven Architectures is and also you will check out all the different components that are part of SAP Integration Suite Advanced Event Management. For example, Issue Control, the Event Portal, and all the different event sources that there are for Event-Driven Architectures in the SAP ecosystem. Be sure to check it out. But what about some hands-on experience for the product? Well, there's also the Event-Reviewing Interpretions with SAP Interpretation Suite CodeGen that you can also request if you want to host an event and that we can get some hands-on experience of the tool. I'm including all the links in the description below.

[Sheena] Hi, everyone. As developers, having a comprehensive overview of syntax and its descriptions in a single document is extremely valuable to us. Andrea Schlotthauer has put together all this information about ABAP Core Data Services in the form of a cheat sheet. This ABAP CDS cheat sheet consists of the overview of all available CDS objects and entities delivered as part of ABAP platform 2023. It also lists down the annotations, associations, data types and type conversions, session variables, and also the operands and expressions that are available in the select list of a Vue entity. In addition, it includes links to numerous knowledge resources for ABAP CDS. So enjoy faster coding by referring to the ABAP CDS cheat sheet linked in the description below.

[Witalij] Hello, it is June already, and it means it's time to recap last month's developer challenge and turn out the new one. During a few weeks of May, you could participate in the developer challenge that was prepared by my dear friend Nora, and you could learn how to use SAP Business AI services, like premium version of document information extraction or data attribute recommendation. A few dozen of you, including actually myself, took part in that with almost 25% going through all five weeks' challenges. Now it's time to announce the new one. So hello, all fellow data geeks, and I'm really happy to announce that this month the challenge will be around SAP HANA multimodal capabilities, including obviously the new Vector engine. The very first week has been published already. And it is all about the setup to get you familiar with SAP Business Application Studio and how it works with Python, Jupyter, and how it can be and should be configured once you are working on a project where you are mining data from SAP HANA Cloud. So I saw the first submission already. It made me really happy and I'm looking for more submissions from all of you.

entry.title: RAP - Unable to navigate to Object Page in FLP but works fine with VS Code
entry.content:
I am pre-filling header and child entities with static factory action and abstract entity. I am filling Reported and Failed Structure if valid date is not entered. If valid data is entered, I am creating header and Child entities and filling Mapped Structure.  While testing, I  gave invalid data and Failed structure is filled fine and errors are shown fine. Now, When I correct the data, the header and child entity is created with Mapped Structure filled fine BUT the navigation to the object is not happening. The dialog screen is getting closed, draft table entry(for both Header and Child) is getting created but no navigation to the object page. 

This issue is only when I am running it in Fiori launchpad. When running locally with VS code, no issue. I see entities created fine and navigation is perfect.

Did anyone come across the issue of navigation in VS code vs FLP? 

#RAP #SAPUI5

entry.title: Re: DDMRP prioritized share
entry.content:
Hi,

The scenario described is very simplistic assuming:

  • DDMRP replenishment taking place at the same time for all plants,
  • The material is buffered in all plants,
  • The supplier is the same for all plants (and there is no supplying alternative for any of the plants).

If you consider that the replenishment run can be plant specific, that the material is not buffered in all plants, and that the (preferred) supplier can differ by plant (which all can be modelled in S/4), the scenario becomes quite complex to handle in business terms.

There is no such prioritized share consideration in SAP S/4HANA Demand-Driven Replenishment taking supplier constraints into consideration.

entry.title: Re: Report for Email address
entry.content: Thank you Donnie, your solution was was useful.

entry.title: Re: ChatGPT Custom Widget: Enhance Your SAP Analytics Cloud Experience with the ChatGPT Custom Widge
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for sharing. 

We are trying to use a deployment from the SAP AI Launchapad (instead of accessing OpenAI) , but we we have issues on cors . Have you faced any similar issue?

Thanks once more.

entry.title: Stock Market News Update: Economic Indicators and Their Impact on Investor Sentiment
entry.content: The stock market is highly sensitive to economic indicators, which are statistics about economic activities and trends that provide insight into the state of the economy. These indicators can significantly impact investor sentiment, influencing stock prices and market movements. Here, we will explore key economic indicators and their recent trends, examining their effects on the stock market.

entry.title: Re: Eclipse: How to see / change function module document...
entry.content: Press F2, click on link.
